theme: How a tech company kept their top 40 engineers from leaving — for $147 per employee per month

linkedin.text:
A CFO kept his 40 best engineers from leaving for $147 each per month. Not a bonus. A trip.

Q1 2025: a 280-person Israeli tech firm was bleeding core IP talent to competitors offering 25-40% more. HR was burning $180k/quarter on retention bonuses — and still losing people.

The CFO's question: "What if we spent that money on something a competitor can't match?"

The 12-month program we built:
→ A 4-day Prague flagship (partners included) for 12 hand-picked engineers
→ Two quarterly micro-retreats, 20-25 people each
→ Family dinners in TLV, plus reserved spots at our 2026 Mallorca flagship

Total: $410k for 40 engineers — $147/employee/month.

12 months later:
→ 0 of 40 left (vs. ~18% industry attrition)
→ eNPS 4.2 → 8.1
→ 3 referral hires ($90k in recruiting saved)
→ Avoided replacement cost: ~$2.1M
→ ROI: 5.1x in year one

The CFO now budgets these trips before bonuses. The wrong event entertains. The right one signals: "You're not replaceable."

📅 Book a free 30-minute call and we'll model the retention math for your team.

#EmployeeEngagement #IncentiveTravel #CorporateEvents #B2BEvents #MICE #Leadership

facebook.text:
A CFO called us in a panic. His 40 best engineers were being poached, and retention bonuses were just buying a few extra months. 💻

We asked one question: what could we give them that a competitor's offer can't?

So instead of another bonus, he spent $147/engineer/month on a year-long experience program — a Prague flagship, micro-retreats, family dinners.

12 months later: 0 of 40 left. eNPS jumped from 4.2 to 8.1. Avoided replacement cost ~$2.1M.

The wrong event entertains. The right one says: "You're not replaceable."

Facing your own retention fight? Send us a message and we'll talk through it. 💬
